steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content:
The trapezoid perimeter is made of $5$ congruent side lengths from the equilateral triangles. Let each side length be $s$.
$$5s=20$$
$$s=4$$

steps.title: Find one triangle area
steps.content:
The area of an equilateral triangle with side length $s$ is
$$\frac{s^2\sqrt{3}}{4}$$
Substitute $s=4$ to find the area of one small triangle.
$$\frac{4^2\sqrt{3}}{4}=4\sqrt{3}$$

steps.title: Double the shaded area
steps.content:
The shaded region consists of $2$ congruent equilateral triangles, so double the area of one triangle.
$$2(4\sqrt{3})=8\sqrt{3}$$
The answer is C.
